title: Understanding WBS Hierarchy and Decomposition
summary: Describes the layered structure of a Work Breakdown Structure, parent‑child relationships, and how to confirm that the WBS is complete.
content: The WBS is organized in levels: Level 1 defines the major deliverable components; each lower level breaks the parent deliverable into finer, noun‑based child deliverables. During decomposition, ask: “Is any solution concept missing?” If yes, add a parent level; if an item is out‑of‑scope, remove it. Continue until every leaf node represents a clear deliverable that can be mapped to activities.
can_answer: What does Level 2 represent in a WBS?
How can you determine if a WBS is complete?
